sessions.title: Competition Awareness Check
sessions.principle:
Competition reveals habits built in training.
Pressure exposes preparation.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we compete with discipline and composure.
Pressure reveals preparation.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Answer honestly: What emotions appear before competition? Which emotions help performance? Which emotions hurt performance?
questions: What emotions appear most before competition?
How do those emotions affect your behavior?

scripture.text: Be strong and courageous.

sessions.title: Preparation Before Competition
sessions.principle:
Preparation improves confidence.
Athletes who prepare well compete calmly and effectively.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, preparation shows respect for competition.
Preparation steadies confidence.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: List three preparation habits before competition.
questions: What should never be skipped before competition?

scripture.text: Prepare your work outside.

sessions.title: Managing Nerves
sessions.principle:
Nervous energy can become focus if controlled.
Discipline turns nervous energy into awareness.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, nerves become focus.
Composure turns energy into awareness.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Describe a healthy way to control nerves before competition.
questions: What routine helps you compete with composure?

scripture.text: Peace I leave with you.

sessions.title: Pre-Match Focus
sessions.principle:
Focus protects preparation.
Distractions weaken performance while focus improves execution.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, focus protects effort.
Focus protects execution.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: List habits that protect focus before competition.
questions: What distracts you most before a match?

scripture.text: Set your minds on things above.

sessions.title: Respecting Competition
sessions.principle:
Competition deserves discipline and respect.
Athletes should respect opponents, officials, and the event.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we respect competition.
Respect strengthens reputation.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Describe respectful behaviors during competition.
questions: What habits build honor in competition?

scripture.text: Show proper respect to everyone.

sessions.title: Representing Your Team
sessions.principle:
Athletes represent their team.
Every behavior reflects the team culture.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, athletes represent the team with honor.
Behavior reflects culture.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes represent their team in competition?
questions: What habits represent the team well?
How should athletes carry themselves publicly?

scripture.text: Let your light shine before others.

sessions.title: Mental Readiness
sessions.principle:
Mental readiness improves performance.
Athletes who prepare mentally react better under pressure.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, mental readiness matters.
Preparation strengthens confidence.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Describe a mental preparation strategy.
questions: How do you prepare your mind before competing?

scripture.text: Be alert and of sober mind.

sessions.title: Competition Responsibility
sessions.principle:
Athletes are responsible for preparation and conduct.
Blaming circumstances weakens growth.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, responsibility belongs to the athlete.
Ownership strengthens discipline.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: List responsibilities athletes carry entering competition.
questions: What belongs to you no matter what?

scripture.text: Each of you should carry your own load.

sessions.title: Pressure Awareness
sessions.principle:
Pressure reveals habits.
Athletes who stay calm perform more consistently.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, pressure reveals discipline.
Calm athletes perform better.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes respond when pressure increases?
questions: What do you return to when pressure rises?

scripture.text: The Lord gives strength to his people.

sessions.title: Staying Calm
sessions.principle:
Calm thinking improves decisions.
Emotion without control weakens performance.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, calm beats chaos.
Calm improves decisions.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What habits help athletes stay calm during competition?

scripture.text: A gentle answer turns away wrath.

sessions.title: Control Under Stress
sessions.principle:
Stress should not control behavior.
Athletes must control reactions under stress.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, discipline controls emotion.
Control protects performance.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why is self-control important during competition?
questions: What happens when emotion takes over?

scripture.text: Better a patient person than a warrior.

sessions.title: Reset After Mistakes
sessions.principle:
Mistakes happen; recovery matters.
Athletes who reset quickly maintain performance.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, mistakes lead to adjustment.
Recovery improves resilience.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes reset after mistakes?
questions: What does a fast recovery look like?

scripture.text: The righteous fall seven times and rise again.

sessions.title: Focus During Competition
sessions.principle:
Focus improves reaction.
Athletes who stay focused adapt faster.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, focus drives execution.
Focus improves performance.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What breaks focus during competition?

scripture.text: Let us fix our eyes on what is unseen.

sessions.title: Adapting Under Pressure
sessions.principle:
Athletes must adapt quickly.
Unexpected moments require calm adjustment.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, athletes adapt with discipline.
Adaptability improves resilience.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes adapt during unexpected situations?

scripture.text: Be wise as serpents and innocent as doves.

sessions.title: Confidence vs Ego
sessions.principle:
Confidence helps; ego harms.
Confidence improves performance while ego weakens judgment.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, confidence replaces ego.
Humility strengthens discipline.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What is the difference between confidence and ego?

scripture.text: Pride goes before destruction.

sessions.title: Maintaining Discipline
sessions.principle:
Discipline must remain under pressure.
Athletes who stay disciplined perform consistently.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, discipline drives performance.
Discipline stabilizes performance.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How can athletes maintain discipline under pressure?
questions: What does discipline look like under pressure?

scripture.text: Train yourself to be godly.

sessions.title: Respecting Opponents
sessions.principle:
Opponents deserve respect regardless of outcome.
Competition challenges athletes but should never destroy respect.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we compete hard and respect opponents.
Respect strengthens character.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why should athletes respect opponents during competition?
questions: Why does respect matter even in hard competition?

scripture.text: Do to others as you would have them do to you.

sessions.title: Fair Competition
sessions.principle:
Integrity matters more than victory.
Winning unfairly destroys the value of competition.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we win with integrity.
Integrity protects honor.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What behaviors show fair competition?

scripture.text: Better a poor man whose walk is blameless.

sessions.title: Learning from Opponents
sessions.principle:
Opponents reveal weaknesses.
Every opponent teaches something valuable.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, opponents help us grow.
Opponents sharpen skill.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What can athletes learn from strong opponents?
questions: What did a strong opponent expose in you?

scripture.text: As iron sharpens iron.

sessions.title: Respecting Officials
sessions.principle:
Officials maintain fairness.
Respecting officials shows maturity and discipline.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we respect the rules and those who enforce them.
Respect strengthens competition.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes treat officials during competition?
questions: What behaviors show respect for officials?
How should athletes respond to decisions they dislike?

scripture.text: Submit to governing authorities.

sessions.title: Sportsmanship in Competition
sessions.principle:
Sportsmanship shows maturity under pressure.
Athletes reveal character not only in how they compete, but in how they carry themselves before, during, and after the result.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, sportsmanship matters.
Sportsmanship reveals discipline beyond performance.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What behaviors show strong sportsmanship during competition?
questions: How do athletes carry themselves after the outcome?

scripture.text: Let your gentleness be evident to all.

sessions.title: Competing Without Hatred
sessions.principle:
Hard competition does not require hostility.
Athletes can compete fiercely without letting contempt or hatred poison their mindset.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, intensity stays clean.
Controlled intensity is stronger than hostility.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How can athletes compete hard without becoming disrespectful or hostile?
questions: How can athletes keep intensity clean?
Why does this matter for character?
What does controlled aggression look like?

scripture.text: Be angry and do not sin.

sessions.title: Learning During Competition
sessions.principle:
Competition itself teaches if athletes stay aware.
Matches reveal patterns that training alone may hide.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, competition teaches us.
Awareness turns experience into growth.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What can athletes learn during competition, not just after it?
questions: What does competition reveal in real time?
How can athletes stay aware while competing?
What patterns appear only under live pressure?
How can you learn while still performing?

scripture.text: Teach me knowledge and good judgment.

sessions.title: Responding to Bad Calls
sessions.principle:
Officials are not under your control.
Your response always is.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we respond with discipline.
Control stays with us.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes respond to bad calls or unfair moments during competition?
questions: Why do bad calls affect athletes so strongly?
How can athletes recover quickly after a bad call?

scripture.text: Better a patient person than a warrior.

sessions.title: Winning with Humility
sessions.principle:
Victory should not create arrogance.
Humility keeps athletes grounded and focused.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, victory is handled with humility.
Humility protects growth.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why is humility important after victory?
questions: Why can winning become dangerous to character?

scripture.text: Pride goes before destruction.

sessions.title: Losing with Discipline
sessions.principle:
Loss reveals character.
Athletes grow when they respond to loss with discipline.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, losses become lessons.
Loss can strengthen discipline.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes respond after losing?
questions: How can discipline turn loss into progress?

scripture.text: Though the righteous fall seven times, they rise again.

sessions.title: Learning from Loss
sessions.principle:
Loss can reveal valuable lessons.
Growth requires honest reflection after defeat.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, losses become teachers.
Honest reflection drives growth.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What lessons can loss teach athletes?
questions: What truth do athletes avoid after losing?

scripture.text: Teach me knowledge and good judgment.

sessions.title: Handling Criticism
sessions.principle:
Criticism can improve athletes.
Athletes must accept helpful feedback.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, feedback builds growth.
Wise athletes learn from correction.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes respond to criticism?
questions: What makes criticism helpful instead of harmful?

scripture.text: Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge.

sessions.title: Maintaining Perspective
sessions.principle:
Competition is one part of development.
Athletes should value growth over ego.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, perspective protects character.
Perspective protects discipline.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why is perspective important after competition?
questions: How does long-term thinking protect character?

scripture.text: The wise store up knowledge.

sessions.title: Returning to Training
sessions.principle:
Competition should lead back to training.
Growth happens through continued work.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, competition leads back to discipline.
Work creates growth.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why should athletes return to training with purpose?
questions: What should athletes improve after competition?
What builds discipline after the event is over?
How does competition point back to work?

scripture.text: Let us not grow weary in doing good.

sessions.title: Winning and Losing in Public
sessions.principle:
Competition is public character testing.
People remember how athletes handle outcomes, not just the outcome itself.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, outcomes never outrank character.
Public response shapes reputation.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes carry themselves publicly after winning or losing?
questions: How should athletes protect character in public?

scripture.text: Let your gentleness be evident to all.

sessions.title: Competing for Growth, Not Applause
sessions.principle:
Athletes lose freedom when they compete for approval instead of truth.
Competition becomes distorted when athletes fight for applause, image, or outside approval instead of honest performance and growth.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we compete for truth, not applause.
Freedom grows when approval loses power.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What changes when an athlete competes for approval instead of growth and performance?
questions: What damage comes from competing for applause?

scripture.text: Am I now trying to win the approval of human beings?

sessions.title: Supporting Teammates
sessions.principle:
Competition is shared with the team.
Teammates support each other through effort and encouragement.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, teammates lift each other.
Support builds unity.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes support teammates in competition?

scripture.text: Carry each other's burdens.

sessions.title: Team Expectations
sessions.principle:
Athletes carry responsibility to their team.
Teams succeed when athletes carry shared responsibility.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, responsibility strengthens teams.
Responsibility builds trust.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What expectations should teams have for competitors?
questions: What should teams be able to count on from competitors?

scripture.text: Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart.

sessions.title: Representing the Team
sessions.principle:
Athletes carry the reputation of the team.
Behavior during competition reflects the team culture.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, athletes represent the team with honor.
Reputation reflects culture.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What behaviors represent a team well?
questions: How do athletes visibly carry the team?

scripture.text: Let your light shine before others.

sessions.title: Leading Through Competition
sessions.principle:
Older or stronger athletes lead by how they carry themselves in competition.
Younger athletes and teammates watch how competitors handle nerves, officials, outcomes, and pressure.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, competitors lead by conduct.
Competition makes leadership visible.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How can an athlete lead others through the way they compete?
questions: What competition behaviors make someone worth following?
What do others learn from you when you compete?
How does leadership show under pressure?

scripture.text: Set an example for the believers.

sessions.title: Protecting the House in Public
sessions.principle:
Competition is one of the clearest places the house is publicly represented.
An athlete does not walk into competition alone. He carries his room, his team, and his standards with him.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we protect the house in public.
Public behavior can strengthen or weaken the house.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What does it mean to protect the house when competing in public?
questions: What public behaviors protect the house?
How do standards stay visible under pressure?

scripture.text: Conduct yourselves in a manner worthy.

sessions.title: Competition Standard Statement
sessions.principle:
Athletes need a clear standard for how they compete.
Without a defined standard, competition becomes emotional, reactive, and inconsistent.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we compete by a clear standard.
Clear standards protect athletes under pressure.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Write your competition standard in one strong sentence.
questions: What should competition always bring out of you?
What standard will guide your competition?
Can someone else clearly see that standard?

scripture.text: Be steadfast in your work.

sessions.title: What Does Honorable Competition Look Like?
sessions.principle:
Honorable competition is disciplined, composed, respectful, and honest.
The strongest competitors are not just dangerous. They are controlled, trustworthy, and grounded in standards under pressure.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, competition stays honorable.
Honor in competition is visible in conduct.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Define honorable competition in actions, not feelings. List 5 behaviors you will live out next time you compete.
questions: What does honorable competition look like?
What would someone else clearly see if you lived this out?

scripture.text: By their fruit you will recognize them.

sessions.title: Competition Honor Retest
sessions.principle:
Competition reveals character growth.
The goal is not image. The goal is composure, discipline, and honor under pressure.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, growth is measured honestly.
Growth becomes visible through reflection.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Repeat Session 1 exactly. Answer again: What emotions appear before competition? Which emotions help performance? Which emotions hurt performance? Then compare your old answer to your new one.

scripture.text: Examine yourselves.
